title: rubus × troyeri
intro: Rubus × troyeri, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, specifically a cross between the Pacific thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus). The medicinal properties of Rubus × troyeri lie in its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, particularly ellagic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × troyeri include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and certain types of cancer. Rubus × troyeri can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried powder, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific preparation and individual needs.
